notes: Base class for all Unity scripts. Inherit to get lifecycle methods (awake, start, update, etc.).
behavioral_differences: Unity uses reflection to find lifecycle methods; Python version uses inheritance and method override
Unity supports partial classes; Python does not

notes: Container for components. Created with GameObject('name'). Has global registry for Find/FindWithTag.
behavioral_differences: Unity GameObjects exist in scenes; Python uses a flat global registry
Unity has SetActive(); Python uses .active bool directly

notes: Every GameObject has a Transform. Stores position (Vector3), rotation (Quaternion), scale. Supports parent-child hierarchy.
behavioral_differences: Unity Transform has localPosition/localRotation separate from world; Python stores world position directly
Position setter accepts Vector2 (converted to Vector3 with z=0) for 2D convenience

notes: 2D physics body wrapping pymunk.Body. Supports dynamic, kinematic, and static body types.
example_unity:
Rigidbody2D rb = GetComponent<Rigidbody2D>();
rb.velocity = new Vector2(5, 0);
behavioral_differences: Unity uses Box2D internally; Python uses pymunk (Chipmunk). Collision resolution differs slightly
Unity drag maps approximately to pymunk damping

notes: Box-shaped 2D collider. Must call build() after setting size and attaching to GameObject.
example_unity:
BoxCollider2D col = gameObject.AddComponent<BoxCollider2D>();
col.size = new Vector2(2, 1);
behavioral_differences: Python requires explicit build() call; Unity auto-builds colliders
Trigger detection uses pymunk sensor shapes

notes: Circle-shaped 2D collider. Must call build() after setting radius.
behavioral_differences: Same as BoxCollider2D — requires explicit build()

notes: Orthographic camera for 2D. First camera created becomes Camera.main. Handles world-to-screen conversion.
behavioral_differences: Unity uses orthographicSize (camelCase); Python uses orthographic_size (snake_case)

notes: Renders a sprite or colored rectangle. Falls back to shape drawing when no sprite assigned.
behavioral_differences: Unity Color is a struct (0-1 floats); Python uses RGB tuple (0-255 ints)
No sprite atlas or animation support

notes: 2D vector backed by numpy. Supports arithmetic, dot, distance, lerp, angle. Static constructors: Vector2.zero, Vector2.up, etc.
example_unity:
Vector2 v = new Vector2(3, 4);
float mag = v.magnitude;

notes: 3D vector backed by numpy. Supports cross product, project, reflect. Static constructors: Vector3.zero, Vector3.forward, etc.
example_unity:
Vector3 v = new Vector3(1, 2, 3);
Vector3 n = v.normalized;

notes: Rotation quaternion backed by pyrr. Supports euler conversion, angle-axis, slerp.
behavioral_differences: Unity Quaternion.Euler is PascalCase; Python is snake_case

notes: Static math utilities. Lerp, Clamp, Approximately, SmoothStep, etc.
